items.text: Did the weakness come on gradually over days, suddenly on one side, or is it spreading upward from your legs?
choices: Gradual, diffuse weakness over days to weeks
Sudden, asymmetric weakness of one side
Symmetric, ascending weakness starting in the legs

items.text: Have you had a fever?
Have you started a new medication or had a dose change in the past few days?
choices: Dose change to an existing chronic medication

items.text: Have you felt like you were going to pass out, or did you actually pass out?
Have you been eating and drinking normally the past few days?
Have you felt unusually cold, gained weight, or become more constipated than usual?
Have you lost weight without trying, or noticed new darkening of your skin?
Have you noticed one side of your face drooping?
Has your speech changed or become slurred?
Have you noticed numbness or tingling on both sides along with the weakness?
Have you been feeling down or lost interest in things you usually enjoy over the past several weeks?
choices: Low mood or anhedonia over several weeks

items.text: Have family or caregivers noticed your attention or thinking coming and going over the past day or two?
choices: Acute inattention or confusion, fluctuating over hours to days

items.text: Who do you have helping you at home, and are you able to get the care and supplies you need?
choices: Signs of self-neglect or suspected abuse or neglect by a caregiver

items.text: Have you fallen recently?
choices: Recent fall with injury or head strike

items.text: Do you have diabetes, and have you felt shaky, sweaty, or hungry along with the weakness?
choices: Known diabetes with tremor, diaphoresis, or hunger

items.text: Have you had a poor diet, heavy alcohol use, weight-loss surgery, or been told you're low in B12?
choices: Poor diet, alcohol use, or bariatric surgery history
Known B12 deficiency with paresthesias or gait imbalance

items.text: Have you lost weight without trying, or do you have a history of cancer with new symptoms?
choices: No unintentional weight loss or known malignancy
Known malignancy with new or worsening symptoms

items.text: Assess vital signs and orthostatic change when safe.
choices: Normal vital signs, no orthostatic change

items.text: Assess attention and level of consciousness.
Assess motor strength for symmetry.
choices: Symmetric distal weakness with diminished reflexes

items.text: Auscultate the heart for rate, rhythm, and murmurs.
choices: Normal rate and regular rhythm, no murmur

items.text: Assess mucous membranes and skin turgor for dehydration.
choices: Dry mucous membranes or poor skin turgor

items.text: Inspect the skin for hyperpigmentation.
Assess the thyroid and for other signs of thyroid dysfunction.
choices: Goiter, dry skin, or delayed reflex relaxation

items.text: Assess proximal muscle strength and safety of gait.
Inspect skin and hygiene for evidence of neglect.
choices: Pressure ulcers or bruising in varying stages of healing

items.text: Perform a rectal examination when indicated.
choices: Normal, guaiac negative, or deferred as not indicated

items.text: Assess deep tendon reflexes and sensation.
choices: Sensory level or stocking-glove sensory loss
